title: capparaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank of Capparaceae refers to the family of flowering plants. Capparaceae is a family of flowering plants that includes around 390 species, commonly known as caper family or shepard's purse family. This family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world and consists of annual and perennial herbs, shrubs, and trees. The Capparaceae family is known for its diverse range of species, including the edible caper plant (Capparis spinosa) and the ornamental plants such as Cleome.
